input.initialPrompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to see what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering, without actually creating the project. This is a template discovery/inspection task. Let me use the template-discovery skill or template-engine agent to find out what files the template would produce.

Actually, I can use `dotnet new` with the `--dry-run` flag to show what would be created without actually creating it.

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1", "description": "Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with server interactivity

data.content:
Here's what `dotnet new blazor` would generate in `./test-output` with `--interactivity Server`:

**App core (17 files):**
- `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `Program.cs`
- `Components/` — `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor`
- `Components/Layout/` — `MainLayout`, `NavMenu`, `ReconnectModal` (each with `.razor` + `.razor.css`, reconnect also has `.razor.js`)
- `Components/Pages/` — `Counter`, `Error`, `Home`, `NotFound`, `Weather` (`.razor`)
- `Properties/launchSettings.json`
- `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json`

**Static assets (51 files):**
- `wwwroot/app.css`, `favicon.png`
- `wwwroot/lib/bootstrap/dist/` — full Bootstrap CSS + JS bundle (grid, reboot, utilities, RTL variants, source maps)

**Post-creation:** NuGet restore would run automatically.

**Total: ~68 files.** Say the word and I'll create it for real.
